message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first ft simulate event-pacing sleep (tick 291)

Migrates the `ft simulate` event-pacing sleep (main.rs L32457) to
`sleep_with_cx` and hoists the per-iteration cx binding above the loop:

- `runtime_compat::sleep(wait)` →
  `sleep_with_cx(&inject_cx, wait)` with Err-handling that breaks the
  scenario event loop on cx-cancel
- Hoists `inject_cx` binding from per-iteration (tick 279) to
  pre-loop — one `Cx::current()` resolve covers N event iterations
  instead of N resolves (tick 249/255 shared-cx pattern).

`ft simulate` plays scenario events at their scheduled `at` offsets
scaled by `speed_factor`. For slow-paced scenarios (100s+ total) a
cx-cancel during pacing had to wait for the current sleep slice to
complete before `inject_with_cx` could observe cancel. Now the sleep
itself is cx-aware: cancel → loop break → scenario returns early.

The `mock.inject_with_cx(&inject_cx, ...)` call (tick 279 migration)
is unchanged — it now reuses the same hoisted cx binding.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 105 (187-291).
main.rs cross-crate migration total: 192 sites (191 before + 1).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 47 (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
